changes: Added import: from src.state_manager import StateManager
Replaced direct JSON file I/O (_state() calls) with StateManager.get_trade_state(sym)
Changed error handling from try-except to defensive None-check
Added detailed completion notes explaining implementation
Documented pattern: StateManager namespace='scanner', get_trade_state(sym), active field check
Documented benefits: consolidated state file, consistent patterns, restart recovery support
Recorded verification: syntax validation, import resolution, logic confirmation

patterns_discovered.name: StateManager Migration Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Systematic replacement of per-symbol file I/O with centralized StateManager.get_trade_state(sym) calls
patterns_discovered.evidence: active_pairs() now instantiates StateManager(namespace='scanner') and iterates through INSTR with single state lookup per symbol
patterns_discovered.consistency: Follows same pattern as scalp_fast.py and scalp_scan.py migrations

patterns_discovered.name: Namespace Reuse Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: All scanner components use namespace='scanner' in StateManager initialization
patterns_discovered.evidence: StateManager(namespace='scanner') instantiation consistent across orchestrate.py, scalp_fast.py, scalp_scan.py
patterns_discovered.benefit: Single shared state file ~/.tv_scanner_state.json for all scanner operations

patterns_discovered.name: Graceful Degradation Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Defensive None-checks replace try-except exception handling for missing state
patterns_discovered.evidence: Changed from try-except json.load pattern to 'if trade_state and trade_state.get("active")'
patterns_discovered.cleaner: StateManager handles file I/O errors internally, function deals only with logic

gotchas_discovered.description: Migration was straightforward with no technical obstacles encountered
gotchas_discovered.reason: StateManager provides clean interface matching required functionality (get_trade_state returns dict or None)

approach_outcome.description: Successfully replaced active_pairs() function to use StateManager for centralized state access
key_achievements: Improved error handling through StateManager abstraction
Maintained backward-compatible function signature and behavior
Consistent with Phase 3 StateManager migration patterns
Enables restart recovery - active trades survives scanner restarts
approach_outcome.code_quality: Improved - cleaner separation of concerns, better testability, reduced file system coupling
approach_outcome.testing: Syntax validation passed, import resolution verified, logic confirmation complete

recommendations.rationale: Phase 4 orchestrate.py migration is on critical path; active_pairs() completion unblocks subsequent tasks

recommendations.action: Add integration test for active_pairs() with StateManager
recommendations.rationale: Verify function correctly retrieves active trades from shared state file in live conditions

recommendations.action: Verify StateManager.get_trade_state() handles edge cases consistently
recommendations.rationale: Missing state file, corrupted JSON, and missing symbols should all return None gracefully across all modules
Ensure future developers understand shared state file expectations and namespace collision rules
